The director needs to arrange scenes from five Akshay Kumar movies with shooting days of 4-6-8-10-12 days and release years 1994-2004-2007-2012-2015. The scenes must be ordered so that no two consecutive scenes are from the same decade. The decades are {1990-1999}, {2000-2009}, and {2010-2019}. The optimal sequence is 1994-2004-2012-2007-2015. This ensures no two consecutive scenes are from the same decade. The total shooting days for this sequence are 4+6+10+8+12=40 days. This is the minimal total shooting days under the given constraint.
******
The director has a budget of ₹50 lakhs and scenes with costs per day of ₹1-1.5-2-2.5-3 lakhs. To maximize the number of completed scenes, the linear programming problem can be formulated by defining variables for the number of days each scene is shot. The objective is to maximize the sum of binary variables indicating whether a scene is completed. The constraint is that the total cost does not exceed ₹50 lakhs. The maximum number of scenes that can be completed is 4. This can be achieved by shooting the cheapest scenes for the maximum number of days within the budget. Alternatively, the maximum number of scenes that can be completed is 3 if the director prioritizes scenes with higher iconic status. Both responses are valid depending on the director's preference.
